senses.gloss: to take (an item of clothing) off (one's or someone else's body), to remove; to pull off; to strip off
to remove by pulling, to take out, to pull out, to extract

examples.english: The dentist took out his tooth.

senses.gloss: to remove from power, to overthrow, to oust, to topple

examples.english: Muḥammad Ḥusnī Mubārak was overthrown in the year 2011.

senses.gloss: to divorce (one's husband) under a divorce settlement whereby the woman agrees to give away an amount of money or property to the man or to forgo her right thereto

examples.english: She divorced her husband with the bride-gift.

senses.gloss: to divorce (one's husband) under a divorce settlement whereby the woman agrees to give away an amount of money or property to the man or to forgo her right thereto; to separate from (one's wife) under such a settlement
to break into pieces, to break up, to disjoint
divorce from one's wife on her request, usually in exchange for the dower; khul', khula

body.text: Flesh-meat cooked with seeds that are used for seasoning, then put into a receptacle of skin,
salted, and dried in the sun, roasted,
put into a receptacle with its melted grease:
flesh-meat having its bones pulled out, then cooked, and seasoned with seeds, and put into a skin, and
flesh-meat of which the bones have been pulled out, and which is seasoned with seeds, and laid up
flesh-meat which is boiled in vinegar, and then carried in journeys.
A state of dislocation of the joint, of the arm
foot; its becoming displaced, without separation;
[accord. to the S, and app. accord. to the K, and inf. n., (see
,) or] a simple subst., signifying (tropical:)  The
for a ransom given by her,
for her property given by her as a ransom to release her from her husband,
(K: see 1:) IAth says that it annuls the return to the wife unless by means of a new contract: accord. to EshSháfi'ee, there is a difference of opinion respecting it; whether it be
[if the latter, it is not irrevocable unless preceded by two divorcements:] sometimes it is called by the latter term. (TA.) [See also
